#Iraq: Basra Oil Company directed operators at West Qurna/2 to suspend pumping entirely and cut production to 50,000 barrels per day effective midnight on June 23, citing the continued absence of available tankers under an ongoing force majeure condition.

According to an internal company document received by Shafaq News, the directive ordered a 100 percent suspension of pumping operations and instructed field operators to store remaining output in on-site tanks. No resumption timeline was specified in the document.

West Qurna/2 is one of Iraq's largest oilfields, located approximately 65 kilometers northwest of Basra in southern Iraq, producing around 460,000 to 480,000 barrels per day and holding an estimated 14 billion barrels of recoverable reserves. The field accounts for nearly 9 percent of Iraq's total crude output.

With no apparent change in the underlying supply and demand outlook, #copper's latest setback appears to be primarily a technically driven correction, amplified by a stronger US dollar and broad-based risk reduction. The move has forced hedge funds and CTAs to reduce a net long position that only recently stood near a five-year high. 

Selling accelerated after the break below the 50-day moving average at USD 6.26/lb before triggering another wave of long liquidation once key support at USD 6.15 - now acting as resistance - gave way. Prices have since stabilised after retracing 50% of the March-to-June rally, with the USD 6.00/lb area attracting renewed interest. 

Unless the stronger dollar continues to weigh on sentiment, the correction increasingly looks like a position reset rather than a deterioration in copper's longer-term fundamental outlook.

Wind Financial Morning Post: June 26, 2026

Market Brief

Fifteen-Fifth Five-Year Plan for a New Energy System takes effect; new energy power generation to account for 30% of total by 2030

Traffic volume through the Strait of Hormuz rebounds significantly; Iran asserts sole management of the strait

U.S. May PCE Price Index rose 4.1% year-over-year, the highest in three years

Tech stocks diverged on Thursday; Micron Technology surged over 15%, Apple fell 6%

Chip costs surge; Apple raises prices globally on multiple products

Storage and memory costs more than double; Microsoft announces Xbox console price hikes

IBM unveils world’s first 0.7-nanometer chip technology, breaking physical limits

Top Stories

Apple Inc. announced price increases for Mac, iPad, home devices, and Vision Pro, with adjustments of up to 20% on multiple models to offset sustained surges in memory and storage chip costs. The price hikes are global. Apple did not raise prices for iPhone, Apple Watch, or AirPods. The company stated it has never witnessed such rapid and substantial increases in component prices and had previously absorbed cost pressures for consumers, but now has no choice but to pass them on.

Hours after Apple announced price hikes for MacBook and iPad, Microsoft also issued a statement that consumers will face higher prices for Xbox gaming consoles due to continued increases in key component costs. Microsoft cited storage and memory prices rising over 2.5 times as the primary driver. Starting August 1, prices for multiple Xbox models will increase. Microsoft also announced it will discontinue sales of the 2TB version of the Xbox Series X launched in 2024.

IBM unveiled the world’s first sub-1-nanometer chip technology, employing a revolutionary 3D “nanostack” architecture that pushes the node to 0.7nm. The new chip doubles transistor density, offering a 50% performance boost or 70% energy efficiency gain compared to 2nm nodes, with mass production expected within five years. This marks the semiconductor industry’s formal entry into the atomic-scale era, surpassing previous physical limits.

Oman’s Foreign Minister Badr stated that future arrangements concerning the Strait of Hormuz will not involve any tolls. Iran estimates that charging for security and environmental services related to the strait could generate $40 billion annually for involved nations. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy emphasized that vessels must coordinate passage in advance, and non-compliant ships “will be dealt with.” S&P Global Energy reported that daily traffic through the Strait of Hormuz in June had recovered to approximately 57% of pre-conflict levels.

The Federal Reserve’s preferred inflation gauge was released. Data from the U.S. Department of Commerce showed the U.S. May PCE Price Index rose 4.1% year-over-year, the highest since April 2023; core PCE increased 3.4% year-over-year, matching expectations and marking the highest level since October 2023. Additionally, the final Q1 real GDP growth was revised up to 2.1% annualized, above the prior estimate of 1.6%. May durable goods orders fell 4.5% month-over-month, the largest decline in nearly a year.

China Macro & Policy

The National Development and Reform Commission and the National Energy Administration issued the “Fifteen-Fifth Five-Year Plan for Building a New Energy System,” aiming for a total energy production capacity of 5.8 billion tons of standard coal by 2030, with coal and oil consumption peaking, non-fossil fuel consumption reaching 25%, non-fossil fuel power generation accounting for 50%, and new energy power generation making up 30% of total output.

Six ministries jointly released a five-year plan for “Beautiful China: Public Action,” outlining 15 specific initiatives across five areas. To promote green lifestyle transitions, the plan targets consumption in food, clothing, housing, transportation, daily use, and tourism, exploring incentive mechanisms such as points redemption, commercial discounts, and public service exchanges.

China’s Ministry of Commerce spokesperson He Yadong said that as of June 2026, over 500 key foreign-invested projects have been launched, including more than 70 flagship projects, primarily in chemical energy, electromechanical equipment, electronic information, automotive, and medical health manufacturing, with total investment exceeding $300 billion.

China Equities Markets

A-shares rose amid persistent AI-driven trading, with semiconductor, computing hardware, and CPO concepts surging, pushing the ChiNext Index and Star 50 Index to all-time highs. At close, the SSE Index gained 0.23% to 4,120.28, the Shenzhen Component Index rose 1.82%, the ChiNext Index jumped 2.84%, the Star 50 Index climbed 3.87%, and the Wind All China Index advanced 0.82%. Market turnover reached 3.62 trillion yuan. Brokerage stocks rose again, while precious metals, oil and gas, chemicals, coal, and non-ferrous metals lagged.

Hong Kong markets declined, with the Hang Seng Index falling 1.43% to 23,076.91 and the Hang Seng Tech Index dropping 1.63%, both hitting their lowest levels in over a year. The Hang Seng China Enterprises Index slid 2.02%. Non-ferrous metals, consumer, and coal stocks underperformed, while semiconductors and CPO concepts surged. Northbound funds sold a net 8.87 billion Hong Kong dollars, with Alibaba hit by a net outflow of 2.25 billion Hong Kong dollars, while Jian Tong Group and SMIC each saw net inflows exceeding 2.6 billion Hong Kong dollars.

The 2026 interim report schedule for Shanghai-listed companies was released. Zhongchuan Special Gases will be first to report on July 18. Haitong Development will report on July 21, while Longruan Technology and Shentong Technology will release results on July 23.

Hong Kong Exchanges and Clearing announced it will optimize strike price intervals for weekly stock and index options to enhance market liquidity. The changes, pending regulatory approval, will take effect on July 27. HKEX also plans to launch T+1 session optimizations on July 20.

China Industry News

Five ministries jointly issued the “Action Plan for Innovative Applications of AI in Transportation,” focusing on pilot demonstrations in autonomous driving, smart highways, intelligent railways, smart shipping, intelligent aviation, smart postal services, smart construction and maintenance, intelligent travel services, smart freight, and intelligent safety supervision.

The global technical regulation for autonomous driving systems (ADS GTR), jointly led by China, the EU, the UK, the U.S., Canada, and Japan, was formally approved and released after voting by all contracting parties.

The State Administration for Market Regulation held its second 2026 corporate fair competition symposium, with representatives from manufacturing, AI, pharmaceuticals, and construction industries discussing “removing bottlenecks to uphold fair market competition.”

The National Energy Administration reported that as of end-May, China’s total installed power generation capacity reached 4.01 billion kilowatts, up 11% year-over-year and the highest globally. Solar capacity hit 1.26 billion kilowatts (+16.3%), and wind capacity reached 660 million kilowatts (+17%).

The National Radio and Television Administration released a classification and tiered management standard for AI-generated short videos, categorizing them as key, ordinary, or other based on investment and subject matter. Projects investing 800,000 yuan or more, or those involving national security themes, are classified as “key short videos.”

A prime residential land parcel in Bao’an District, Shenzhen, was auctioned with an opening price of 5.28 billion yuan. After over 300 rounds of bidding, Poly secured the land for a total of 10.51 billion yuan, a premium of over 99%, with a floor price of 87,900 yuan per square meter, ranking among the top three in Shenzhen’s residential land price history.

Fintech

U.S. Democratic members of the House of Representatives urged the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to respond to regulatory questions regarding AI trading agents by July 31. These tools, currently operating outside existing securities regulations, can make major investment decisions on behalf of retail investors and are expanding from cryptocurrency into options and futures markets.

Corporate News & Updates

Sources say Apple is making its largest-ever strategic adjustment to its Mac chip development, planning to launch a new AI-focused processor line for next-generation high-end chips. Insiders revealed Apple’s current chip iteration is the M5 series, with a basic M6 processor expected as early as this year for entry-level Mac devices.

DeepSeek posted job ads stating that as technology evolves, it aims to expand the size of all departments by at least 100%. Open positions include server-side development engineers, pre-training data engineers, AI search algorithm/architecture engineers, Agent Harness team, Agent Infra engineers, front-end/client-side developers, and AI cross-disciplinary talent.

Hurun Research Institute’s “2026 Global Unicorn Index” showed global unicorn companies reached a record high of 1,603, up 80 from last year. Anthropic, OpenAI, and ByteDance ranked first, second, and third with valuations of 6.6 trillion yuan, 5.8 trillion yuan, and 3.3 trillion yuan, respectively. DeepSeek ranked in the top 15 with a valuation of 340 billion yuan.

TSMC forecasted that global semiconductor market revenue will surpass $1 trillion this year and reach $1.5 trillion by 2030. TSMC expects demand to be driven primarily by high-performance computing and AI, accounting for 55% of total market; followed by smartphones (20%), automobiles (10%), and IoT (10%).

Huawei’s Harmony Intelligent Mobility unveiled the Zunjie S800 Grand Design Collector’s Edition, with a pre-sale price range of 1.6 to 2 million yuan, targeting the ultra-luxury vehicle market above 2 million yuan. Sales data show the current Zunjie S800 has delivered 19,000 units and has topped the million-yuan luxury vehicle sales chart for nine consecutive months.

The U.S. Supreme Court ruled 7-2 that consumers cannot sue Bayer over its Roundup herbicide for failing to warn of cancer risks. The ruling is expected to end nearly a decade of litigation that has cost Bayer over $10 billion.

Amazon announced it will increase its investment in India by $13 billion to expand AI and cloud infrastructure, boosting AWS data center capacity in Mumbai and Hyderabad.

Global Macro & Policy

The U.S. Supreme Court issued two key rulings supporting Trump administration immigration policies. One allowed the administration to rescind temporary protected status for hundreds of thousands of immigrants. Another permitted enforcement of policies blocking asylum seekers from entering U.S. territory.

New York Fed President John Williams said interest rates are at a level conducive to bringing inflation back toward the central bank’s target. He expects inflation to ease to 3.5% by year-end, then gradually decline to 2% by 2028.

The U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) is updating regulations to eliminate the requirement that autonomous vehicles must be equipped with brake pedals, lowering regulatory barriers for Tesla, Waymo, and others seeking to expand autonomous taxi deployments in the U.S. Other vehicles will still require brake pedals.

The U.S. Department of Labor reported that initial jobless claims fell by 12,000 to 215,000 for the week ending June 20, below the forecast of 225,000. Continuing claims rose slightly to 1.821 million, above the expected 1.8 million.

Haruhiko Tamura, a hawkish member of the Bank of Japan’s policy board, explicitly called for interest rate hikes every few months, aiming to gradually raise policy rates toward the estimated 2% neutral rate. He added that if inflation risks intensify, the central bank “must not hesitate to accelerate the pace of hikes.”

The European Council formally approved implementation of tariff commitments under the EU-U.S. Joint Statement, endorsing the trade agreement before the deadline set by U.S. President Trump.

Global Equity Markets

U.S. major indices closed mixed: the Dow Jones rose 0.14% to 51,920.62, the S&P 500 edged down 0.01% to 7,357.49, and the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.46% to 25,358.60. Apple shares dropped 6.12%, its steepest decline since April 2025. Storage chip stocks surged: SanDisk rose nearly 22%, Micron Technology gained over 15%, Applied Materials climbed over 13%, Western Digital rose nearly 5%, ASML advanced over 4%, and Qualcomm rose over 3%. The Wind US Magnificent Seven Index declined 2.75%, with Microsoft and Amazon each falling over 3%, and Meta down over 2%. The Nasdaq China Golden Dragon Index dropped 2.7%, with Dindong Buycai falling over 8%.

European indices closed higher: Germany’s DAX rose 1.03% to 24,994.83, France’s CAC 40 gained 0.55% to 8,431.61, and the UK’s FTSE 100 climbed 0.65% to 10,529.89. Eurozone economic data exceeded expectations: manufacturing PMI rose to 47.8 in June, while services PMI held steady at 52.3, signaling easing recession risks and improving market sentiment.

AI-driven trading revived, sending Japanese and South Korean markets sharply higher. Japan’s Nikkei 225 surged 4.61% to 72,366.34, a new closing record. South Korea’s KOSPI rose 5.42% to 8,930.30, triggering a circuit breaker during trading. AI stocks soared: SK Hynix jumped 13%, Kioxia gained over 12%, SoftBank rose nearly 8%, and Samsung Electronics climbed over 5%. India’s S&P BSE Sensex edged up 0.14% to 77,100.47.

ETF independent research firm ETFGI reported that as of end-May, global actively managed ETF assets reached a record $2.49 trillion. In May, global active ETFs attracted net inflows of $100.08 billion, bringing year-to-date inflows to $411.75 billion, far exceeding the $220.53 billion for all of 2025.

Sources indicate OpenAI prefers to delay its IPO until next year.

Bonds

China’s interbank bond market showed renewed strength, with major government bond yields generally declining, especially for one-year and seven-year tenors. Treasury futures mostly rose, with the 30-year contract leading gains at 0.33%. The People’s Bank of China net injected 122.5 billion yuan via seven-day reverse repos, coupled with a 200-billion-yuan rollover of MLF, improving liquidity conditions. The PBOC also announced it will add overnight reverse repo operations on June 29 and June 30.

U.S. Treasury yields moved mixed: the two-year yield fell 2.05 basis points to 4.121%, the three-year yield dropped 1.41 basis points to 4.125%, the five-year yield declined 0.52 basis points to 4.165%, the 10-year yield rose 0.20 basis points to 4.392%, and the 30-year yield climbed 2.05 basis points to 4.861%.

Commodities

U.S. crude oil futures rose 1.61% to $71.47 per barrel; Brent crude climbed 1.65% to $75.09 per barrel. The rally followed a sharp prior decline and was supported by unexpected U.S. crude inventory draws.

Precious metals closed mixed: COMEX gold futures gained 0.82% to $4,041.60 per ounce; COMEX silver futures fell 0.34% to $57.89 per ounce. Fed’s persistent inflation control with delayed target timing, IMF policy endorsement, and weakening geopolitical risk sentiment, combined with record-low domestic inventory, drove divergent price movements.

Most base metals on the LME rose: aluminum gained 2.02% to $3,185.50 per tonne, copper advanced 1.75% to $13,316.00 per tonne, tin rose 0.94% to $50,150.00 per tonne, zinc climbed 0.48% to $3,438.00 per tonne, nickel edged up 0.28% to $16,865.00 per tonne, and lead dipped 0.03% to $1,912.50 per tonne.

As June 30 approaches, global AI computing metals face new volatility. The U.S. Section 232 tariff review will conclude, and expectations of new copper tariffs have already disrupted markets. If implemented, Goldman Sachs and other institutions expect a fresh U.S. stockpiling surge. COMEX inventories have climbed above 650,000 tonnes, a historical high. Tin, tantalum, and indium—closely tied to AI computing—may see further price catalysts.

An Iraqi oil ministry official told media that if Iraq’s oil production quota is not significantly increased, the government is “considering all feasible options, including exiting OPEC.” The ministry later clarified that the government has never proposed exiting OPEC and that the report does not reflect official policy.

China Construction Bank announced it will shut down its agency individual precious metals trading services with the Shanghai Gold Exchange effective at the end of day on July 24.

FX & Currency Markets

On Thursday, the onshore yuan closed at 6.7995 per U.S. dollar, up 57 basis points from the previous close; the offshore yuan closed at 6.7990. The PBOC’s daily reference rate was set at 6.8209, a 14-basis-point depreciation from the prior day.

At New York close, the U.S. dollar index fell 0.12% to 101.46. Most non-U.S. currencies rose: the euro gained 0.10% to 1.1370 against the dollar, the pound rose 0.18% to 1.3192, the Australian dollar climbed 0.15% to 0.6911, the dollar-yen held flat at 161.8045, the dollar-Canadian dollar fell 0.23% to 1.4200, the dollar-franc dropped 0.26% to 0.8102, and the offshore yuan rose 116 basis points to 6.8016 against the dollar.

Key Economic Events

04:00 EDT, Jun 26: ECB 1-year/3-year CPI expectations for May in Europe

05:00 EDT, Jun 26: Italy Istat Consumer Confidence Index for June

08:30 EDT, Jun 26: U.S. Wholesale Inventories for May

10:00 EDT, Jun 26: U.S. University of Michigan Final Consumer Confidence Index for June

10:00 EDT, Jun 26: U.S. Final 1-year Inflation Expectations for June

01:00 EDT, Jun 27: U.S. Weekly Oil Drilling Rig Count through Jun 26

22:00 EDT, Jun 25: State Council Information Office holds press conference on plans for accelerating the construction of a new energy system during the 15th Five-Year Plan period

03:00 EDT, Jun 26: National Health Commission holds press conference on promoting reproductive health

09:45 EDT, Jun 26: ECB Executive Board member Schnabel speaks

11:30 EDT, Jun 26: 2026 FOMC voter and Minneapolis Fed President Kashkari speaks

24:00 EDT, Jun 26: ECB Deputy Governor Vujčić speaks

2026 China (Shenzhen) Integrated Circuit Summit convened

2026 Beijing International Low-altitude Economy and UAV Systems Industry Exhibition to be held from Jun 26 to 28

New Zealand Reserve Bank Governor Breman attends the 25th Annual Meeting of the Bank for International Settlements (BIS)

[New Listings] Lingyi Smart Manufacturing H-shares, Ketao Shares H-shares, Shengbang Shares H-shares, Xincheng Microequipment H-shares, Zhongke Wenge, MERDEKAGOLD-DRS listed on HKEX; Huake Shares listed on SZSE

MARKET UPDATE — THURSDAY, 25 JUNE 2026

The AI rally has survived its first major inflation test.

Micron delivered extraordinary earnings, semiconductor shares surged globally and Nasdaq futures climbed more than 2%.

US inflation then rose above 4% for the first time in three years—but the monthly increase was slightly softer than feared.

The result is a delicate market balance:

AI profits are accelerating.

The economy remains resilient.

But inflation is still far too high for the Federal Reserve.

🟢 MICRON REIGNITES THE AI RALLY

Micron reported one of the most remarkable earnings expansions of the AI boom.

Fiscal third-quarter results included:

🔸 Revenue: $41.46 billion

🔸 Year-on-year revenue growth: approximately 346%

🔸 Net income: $28.24 billion

🔸 Adjusted earnings per share: $25.11

🔸 Adjusted gross margin: 84.9%

🔸 Adjusted free cash flow: $18.3 billion

For comparison, Micron generated only $9.3 billion of revenue and $1.9 billion of net income during the same quarter last year.

The company expects the acceleration to continue.

Its fourth-quarter guidance included:

🔸 Revenue of approximately $50 billion

🔸 Adjusted gross margin of roughly 86%

🔸 Adjusted earnings of approximately $31 per share

Micron shares surged approximately 17%–18% before the opening bell.

Why does this matter?

Micron manufactures memory chips.

These chips work alongside processors from companies such as Nvidia and AMD, allowing AI systems to store and rapidly access enormous quantities of data.

Think of an AI processor as a brilliant chef.

The memory system is the kitchen counter.

The chef may be exceptionally fast, but without enough working space, the entire kitchen slows down.

AI companies are therefore rushing to secure advanced memory—and Micron is benefiting from an extraordinary shortage.

🟢 THE MEMORY SHORTAGE MAY LAST BEYOND 2027

Micron’s management expects tight memory-market conditions to persist beyond 2027.

The company has secured 16 long-term strategic agreements with major customers, some lasting between three and five years.

Several customers are even providing billions of dollars in advance payments to secure future supplies.

That matters because memory chips have historically experienced violent boom-and-bust cycles.

Manufacturers expand production when prices are high.

Too much capacity eventually appears.

Prices then collapse.

Micron’s long-term agreements may make this cycle more durable by locking in customer demand before new factories are completed.

Susquehanna estimates that Micron’s annual free cash flow could rise from approximately $49.5 billion during the current fiscal year to more than $110 billion during the following year.

That is an analyst forecast—not company guidance.

But it explains why investors increasingly view Micron as more than a temporary beneficiary of high memory prices.

The market is beginning to ask whether Micron has become a genuine long-term cash-generation machine.

🟢 GLOBAL CHIP STOCKS SURGE

Micron’s results quickly spread across global markets.

Japan’s Nikkei 225 rose approximately 4.6%.

🔸 Kioxia gained roughly 12%

South Korea’s Kospi climbed approximately 5.4%, reversing much of Tuesday’s severe semiconductor sell-off.

🔸 SK Hynix rose approximately 13%

🔸 Samsung Electronics also advanced strongly

In US premarket trading:

🔸 Qualcomm: approximately +12%

🔸 Sandisk: approximately +13%

🔸 Western Digital: more than +12%

The message from investors was clear:

The AI trade still has strong fundamental support.

That does not mean technology valuations are cheap.

It means the demand behind the AI infrastructure boom remains real.

A speculative bubble can rise on excitement alone.

Micron’s results show that at least some companies are now converting that excitement into extraordinary revenue, margins and cash flow.

🟢 SK HYNIX PREPARES FOR A HISTORIC US LISTING

SK Hynix shares received support from two separate developments.

First, Micron’s results showed that global demand for memory chips remains exceptionally strong.

Second, SK Hynix announced plans to list American depositary receipts on Nasdaq.

In simple terms, this would allow US investors to buy SK Hynix shares more easily through the American stock market.

The company reportedly intends to:

🔸 Begin Nasdaq trading on 10 July

🔸 Raise approximately $29 billion–$30 billion

🔸 Broaden access to US and international investors

The transaction could become one of the largest international share offerings in history.

For Micron, this creates both an opportunity and a risk.

Greater attention on SK Hynix could attract more capital towards the entire memory-chip sector.

But it would also give American investors easier access to one of Micron’s largest competitors.

Think of Micron as the main memory-chip shop on a busy American street.

A Nasdaq listing would allow SK Hynix to open a large shop next door.

That could bring more customers to the area—but it would also increase competition for their money.

🟢 US INFLATION RISES ABOVE 4%

The Federal Reserve’s preferred inflation gauge accelerated again in May.

HEADLINE PCE

🔸 Monthly: +0.4%

🔸 Forecast: +0.5%

🔸 Annual: +4.1%

🔸 Previous annual rate: +3.8%

CORE PCE

🔸 Monthly: +0.3%

🔸 Annual: +3.4%

🔸 Previous annual rate: +3.3%

Headline inflation has now exceeded 4% for the first time since April 2023.

The good news was that the monthly headline increase was slightly softer than the 0.5% economists expected.

The bad news was that underlying inflation continued rising.

Core PCE excludes volatile food and energy prices and provides a clearer view of persistent inflation.

Its increase to 3.4% shows that the problem is not confined entirely to oil.

Simple explanation:

The inflation report was not disastrous—but it was not comfortable either.

The monthly headline figure was slightly better than feared.

But the annual figures show that prices are still rising at more than twice the Federal Reserve’s 2% target.

🟢 WHY MARKETS DID NOT PANIC

Despite inflation reaching 4.1%, US stock futures extended their gains after the release.

Shortly after the data:

🔸 Nasdaq 100 futures: approximately +2.3%

🔸 S&P 500 futures: approximately +0.8%

🔸 Dow futures: approximately +0.3%

🔸 US Dollar Index: broadly unchanged near 101.6

There are three reasons markets remained calm.

First, monthly headline inflation was slightly softer than expected.

Second, much of May’s increase reflected the earlier oil shock caused by the Iran war.

Oil prices have since fallen dramatically.

Third, Micron’s earnings provided powerful evidence that AI spending is producing genuine profits.

The market therefore interpreted the data as:

“Inflation is high, but it was not worse than feared—and the AI earnings story remains exceptionally strong.”

🟢 THE REPORT DOES NOT REMOVE FED RATE-RISE RISK

Investors should not mistake a positive stock-market reaction for a dovish inflation report.

Core inflation remains at 3.4%.

Consumer spending is strong.

Income growth is strong.

Business investment is resilient.

Jobless claims remain low.

GDP was revised higher.

That combination gives the Federal Reserve little urgency to reduce interest rates.

It may also leave the door open for another rate increase if inflation remains elevated.

Micron has strengthened the AI earnings story.

But persistent inflation could still limit how much investors are willing to pay for that growth.

🟢 US CONSUMERS KEEP SPENDING

Personal spending increased 0.7% in May.

Economists expected an increase of approximately 0.5%–0.6%.

Personal income also rose 0.7%, beating expectations of roughly 0.4%–0.5%.

Disposable income increased by the same amount.

The personal saving rate recovered from 2.6% to 3%.

This is broadly positive for economic growth.

Consumers earned more, spent more and saved a slightly larger share of their income.

However, part of the income improvement may have come from temporary agricultural-support payments.

The saving-rate rebound should therefore not automatically be viewed as a lasting improvement in household finances.

For the Federal Reserve, stronger spending creates another complication.

Consumers are absorbing higher prices rather than cutting expenditure sharply.

That keeps economic growth alive—but may also allow companies to continue raising prices.

🟢 GDP IS REVISED HIGHER—BUT THE DETAILS ARE WEAKER

First-quarter US economic growth was revised from 1.6% to 2.1% annualised.

That was considerably stronger than expected.

At first glance, the revision suggests the US economy entered the year with more momentum than previously believed.

But the details were less impressive.

The improvement mainly reflected lower imports, which mechanically increase measured GDP.

Meanwhile:

🔸 Real consumer-spending growth was revised down to only 0.5%

🔸 Final sales to private domestic purchasers rose 1.7%

That measure strips out volatile trade, inventory and government components and provides a cleaner view of underlying domestic demand.

The message is therefore mixed.

Headline GDP was stronger.

But the economy’s internal engine was not as powerful as the headline suggests.

🟢 BUSINESS INVESTMENT LOOKS HEALTHIER THAN THE HEADLINE

Headline durable-goods orders fell 4.5% in May after rising 7.9% previously.

That sounds weak.

But the decline was heavily influenced by volatile transportation and defence orders.

The underlying figures were much stronger:

🔸 Durable goods excluding transportation: +1.3%

🔸 Forecast: +0.6%

🔸 Core capital-goods orders excluding aircraft: +1.6%

🔸 Forecast: +0.6%

Core capital-goods orders are often treated as a measure of business-investment intentions.

Their strong increase suggests companies are continuing to spend on equipment despite higher borrowing costs.

That is positive for industrial companies and economic growth.

But it also reinforces the view that the economy may be too resilient to justify lower interest rates.

🟢 THE LABOUR MARKET REMAINS RESILIENT

Initial jobless claims fell to 215,000.

🔸 Forecast: 225,000

🔸 Previous: 227,000

Fewer new claims suggest companies are still reluctant to dismiss workers.

However, continuing claims increased to 1.821 million, slightly above expectations.

That means fewer people are losing jobs—but those who do lose employment may be taking longer to find another position.

The four-week average of claims also edged higher to 224,250.

The labour market is therefore cooling gradually at the edges.

But there is still no evidence of a serious breakdown.

For the Federal Reserve, that resilience provides room to keep policy restrictive while it fights inflation.

🟢 MAY may STILL REPRESENT PEAK INFLATION

May’s inflation data measure the economy before the latest collapse in oil prices.

The Iran war drove energy and transportation costs sharply higher earlier in the year.

Since then, Brent crude has fallen from approximately $126 at its March peak to around $73.

That means today’s inflation report is partly like looking through the rear-view mirror.

It shows where inflation was before energy prices reversed—not necessarily where headline inflation is heading next.

Cheaper oil should gradually reduce:

🔸 Fuel costs

🔸 Transportation expenses

🔸 Manufacturing costs

🔸 Pressure on household budgets

🔸 Headline inflation

But lower oil cannot solve every inflation problem.

Services inflation remains sticky.

Consumer demand remains resilient.

And the enormous AI infrastructure build-out is creating a new source of price pressure.

🟢 AI MAY BE CREATING A THIRD INFLATION WAVE

The AI boom is not merely a software story.

It requires physical infrastructure:

🔸 Memory chips

🔸 Advanced processors

🔸 Data centres

🔸 Cooling systems

🔸 Electrical equipment

🔸 Fibre-optic networks

🔸 Backup generators

🔸 Construction workers

🔸 Enormous quantities of electricity

Five major hyperscalers—Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, Microsoft and Oracle—are expected to spend approximately $741 billion this year, nearly 75% more than last year.

Estimated AI infrastructure investment through 2032 could reach approximately $8 trillion.

That demand is already pushing prices higher.

🔸 Consumer computer software and accessory prices rose around 15% year-on-year in May.

🔸 Wholesale electronic-component prices increased approximately 27%.

🔸 Wages for electrical and wiring contractors rose 6.5%, compared with 3.6% across the broader private sector.

🔸 Data centres may account for almost half of US electricity-demand growth through 2030.

This creates a paradox.

In the long term, AI could reduce inflation by helping workers and companies produce more with fewer resources.

But in the short term, building the infrastructure may do the opposite.

It is like constructing a railway.

Once completed, the railway makes transportation faster and cheaper.

But during construction, it consumes steel, workers, land and capital—pushing costs higher before the benefits arrive.

AI may eventually become disinflationary.

For now, the infrastructure race is inflationary.

🟢 APPLE RAISES PRICES AS “CHIPFLATION” SPREADS

Apple has increased prices across much of its MacBook and iPad range by approximately 20%.

Examples include:

🔸 512GB MacBook Air: $1,099 → $1,299

🔸 256GB iPad Pro: $999 → $1,199

🔸 Entry-level MacBook Neo: increased by as much as 25% in some reported pricing

Apple has not raised iPhone prices for now.

The company blamed unprecedented increases in memory and storage costs caused by the AI data-centre boom.

Micron, SK Hynix and Samsung are prioritising advanced high-bandwidth memory used by AI systems.

That leaves less manufacturing capacity available for traditional consumer products.

Apple’s price rises are important because they provide real-world evidence of the AI inflation problem.

AI companies are willing to pay enormous premiums for scarce memory.

Consumer-electronics companies must then choose between:

🔸 Accepting lower profit margins

🔸 Raising prices

🔸 Reducing production

Apple has chosen to pass part of the cost to customers.

Micron’s gain is becoming the consumer’s bill.

🟢 QUALCOMM ADDS TO THE AI OPTIMISM

Qualcomm shares jumped approximately 12% after the company raised its revenue guidance and announced a partnership with Meta.

The companies plan to develop central processing units for Meta’s infrastructure.

The announcement broadens Qualcomm’s growth story beyond smartphones and towards data centres.

Together, Micron and Qualcomm strengthened the argument that the AI boom is spreading across a wider semiconductor ecosystem.

🟢 IBM ANNOUNCES A MAJOR CHIP BREAKTHROUGH

IBM unveiled what it described as the world’s first sub-one-nanometre chip technology.

Its new 0.7-nanometre—or seven-angstrom—design uses a three-dimensional “nanostack” architecture.

IBM says the technology could offer:

🔸 Up to 50% higher performance

🔸 Up to 70% greater energy efficiency

🔸 Nearly 100 billion transistors on a fingernail-sized chip

IBM shares rose approximately 5%–6% before the opening.

However, investors should understand what was announced.

This is a major research and manufacturing breakthrough—not a chip entering mass production today.

Commercial production could still take several years.

Its significance is long term:

Smaller and more efficient chips could reduce the electricity required for AI and data centres.

That may eventually help relieve one of the AI boom’s largest economic constraints—power consumption.

🟢 OIL FALLS BACK TOWARDS PREWAR LEVELS

Oil continued its extraordinary reversal.

Early Thursday:

🔸 Brent crude: approximately $73 per barrel

🔸 WTI crude: approximately $69–$70

Brent briefly traded near or below the $72.48 closing level recorded immediately before the Iran war began.

That represents a dramatic decline from its March peak near $126.

Why is oil falling?

Tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is recovering much faster than many traders expected.

At least 20 million barrels reportedly left the strait during one 24-hour period.

Thirty-one tankers exited the Gulf on Wednesday—nearly 50% more than the previous day.

Goldman Sachs estimates Gulf oil exports have recovered to approximately 63% of normal levels.

The market is also anticipating:

🔸 Further reopening of Hormuz

🔸 Restarted Gulf production

🔸 Temporary sanctions relief for Iranian oil

🔸 The release of oil previously trapped inside the Gulf

🔸 Possible short-term oversupply

Falling oil is the strongest argument that May may represent the peak in headline inflation.

🟢 THE OIL DECLINE MAY HAVE GONE TOO FAR

Despite the optimism, the energy situation is not fully normal.

More than one billion barrels were reportedly trapped inside the Gulf during the conflict.

The current export surge partly represents delayed oil suddenly reaching the market—not permanently higher production.

Production facilities still need time to restart.

Shipping routes have not fully normalised.

Global inventories have also been heavily depleted.

Some analysts therefore believe the market has become oversold.

Energy Aspects believes crude could eventually establish a floor closer to $80–$90 after the immediate release of trapped oil passes through the market.

Macquarie is more bearish, forecasting third-quarter averages near:

🔸 Brent: $67

🔸 WTI: $62

The disagreement highlights the central risk.

Oil’s short-term direction is being driven by a wave of delayed supply.

Its medium-term direction will depend on Gulf production, inventory rebuilding and whether the peace agreement survives.

🟢 GOLD AND BITCOIN REMAIN UNDER PRESSURE

Gold remains near or below $4,000 an ounce.

Bitcoin is hovering just above $61,000 after falling to $59000 on Wednesday.

Both assets have been pressured by expectations that interest rates may remain high or rise further.

Gold does not pay interest.

When government-bond yields rise, investors can earn higher returns from safer assets, making gold relatively less attractive.

Bitcoin currently behaves partly as a liquidity-sensitive risk asset.

When monetary policy tightens, speculative capital becomes more expensive and investors often reduce exposure.

Today’s inflation report offered some relief because monthly headline inflation was softer than forecast.

But the strong income, spending, employment and business-investment figures prevent the report from being clearly bullish for either asset.

Strategy shares recovered approximately 3% before the opening after falling 9.4% on Wednesday.

The rebound is modest compared with the strength in semiconductor shares.

For now, AI—not crypto—is absorbing the market’s risk appetite.

🟢 CEREBRAS SHOWS THE OTHER SIDE OF THE AI BOOM

Not every AI company is being rewarded.

Cerebras Systems fell almost 20% after reporting its first quarterly results as a public company.

The company delivered a smaller-than-expected loss but warned that gross margins would decline as it expands a large service contract with OpenAI.

Its shares fell below the $185 IPO price and approximately 41% below the peak reached during its first trading day.

This provides an important contrast with Micron.

The market is no longer rewarding every company merely for having an AI connection.

Investors increasingly want:

🔸 Revenue growth

🔸 Strong margins

🔸 Positive cash flow

🔸 Durable customer demand

🔸 A credible route to profitability

Micron delivered those ingredients.

Cerebras raised concerns about margins.

The AI trade is becoming more selective.

🟢 DEFENCE SPENDING REMAINS STRUCTURALLY STRONG

Lockheed Martin secured a seven-year US government contract worth up to $35 billion for Terminal High Altitude Area Defence interceptor missiles.

The award follows plans to increase Patriot missile production.

The contracts reinforce a broader structural investment theme.

Even if fighting in the Middle East eases, governments are rebuilding missile and air-defence inventories depleted by recent conflicts.

Peace may lower near-term oil prices.

It does not necessarily mean defence spending will fall.

🟢 GLOBAL CENTRAL BANKS REMAIN DIVIDED

The world is not experiencing one uniform interest-rate cycle.

Different economies face very different combinations of inflation and growth.

AUSTRALIA

Australia added 40,300 jobs in May, exceeding expectations of approximately 32,000.

🔸 Unemployment fell from 4.5% to 4.4%

🔸 Full-time employment rose by 5,200

🔸 Part-time employment increased by 35,200

🔸 Underemployment edged up to 5.9%

Underlying inflation reached 3.6% in May, above the Reserve Bank of Australia’s 2%–3% target.

The resilient labour market therefore keeps another Australian rate rise possible.

JAPAN

Bank of Japan board member Naoki Tamura argued that underlying inflation has already reached the BOJ’s 2% target.

He believes rates should move steadily towards a neutral level near 2%, potentially through increases of 0.25 percentage points every few months.

The BOJ raised its policy rate to 1% last week—the highest level in 31 years.

After decades of fighting deflation, Japan is now debating how rapidly to tighten monetary policy without damaging growth.

CANADA

The Bank of Canada kept its policy rate at 2.25%.

Policymakers are trying not to overreact to energy-driven inflation because:

🔸 The economy remains weak

🔸 Labour-market slack persists

🔸 Higher oil costs have not spread widely through other prices

🔸 Economic output contracted during two consecutive quarters

Falling oil further reduces the immediate need for tighter Canadian policy.

🟢 WHAT HAPPENS NEXT

The immediate data risk has passed without derailing the AI rally.

But several catalysts remain today:

🔸 Federal Reserve officials Michelle Bowman, John Williams and Austan Goolsbee are scheduled to speak.

🔸 The US Treasury will auction $44 billion of seven-year notes.

The bond auction matters.

Weak demand would force the Treasury to offer investors higher yields.

That could place renewed pressure on technology shares, gold and Bitcoin—even after Micron’s exceptional earnings.

Fed officials will also help determine whether policymakers interpret today’s data as:

🔸 Evidence that inflation may be peaking

or

🔸 Evidence that strong demand will keep inflation elevated

🟢 WHAT IT MEANS FOR INVESTORS

Today’s market contains four competing forces.

1️⃣ MICRON SUPPORTS THE AI TRADE

Explosive revenue, margins and cash flow show that AI infrastructure demand is genuine.

This supports memory manufacturers, semiconductor-equipment companies and much of the broader AI supply chain.

2️⃣ INFLATION IS HIGH—BUT NOT WORSE THAN FEARED

Headline PCE reached 4.1%, but the monthly increase was slightly below expectations.

That was enough to prevent the data from disrupting the technology rally.

3️⃣ THE ECONOMY REMAINS TOO RESILIENT FOR EASY MONEY

Spending, income, business investment and the labour market were stronger than expected.

The Federal Reserve has little reason to cut rates and may still raise them if core inflation remains sticky.

4️⃣ FALLING OIL MAY BRING FUTURE RELIEF

The rapid reversal in energy prices should weaken headline inflation during the coming months.

But it will not automatically solve services inflation or the new demand pressures created by AI infrastructure.

The market is therefore balancing two truths:

The AI boom is stronger than the bears feared.

But the stronger it becomes, the more pressure it may place on chips, electricity, consumer prices, interest rates and valuations.

📌 THE TAKEAWAY

🔸 Micron has proved that the AI boom is producing extraordinary real-world demand—not merely speculative excitement.

🔸 The PCE report showed that inflation remains dangerously elevated, but it was not sufficiently hot to derail the AI rally.

🔸 Strong spending, income, investment and employment suggest the US economy is not approaching an immediate recession.

🔸 Falling oil should ease future headline inflation, although core pressures remain persistent.

🔸 Apple’s price increases show that the AI infrastructure boom is already passing costs from data centres to ordinary consumers.

This is not a simple “risk-on” signal.

It is a battle between exceptional corporate growth and restrictive monetary conditions.

For now, Micron is winning that battle.

But the next phase of the market will depend on whether AI profits can continue growing faster than inflation, interest rates and investor expectations.

That is precisely the bridge between what the suits want and what the cypherpunks built, and it might be the thing that actually lets both worlds coexist. Quantstamp also formally confirmed the four-year-old Zcash Orchard counterfeiting bug, with no exploitation found, which closes that chapter cleanly. Zcash $413. Monero $313, down 2.5%, still the sector anchor.

Bittensor $220, the decentralized AI anchor holding firm. And the AI story of the day feeds the thesis perfectly: Anthropic accused Alibaba of illicitly extracting Claude’s capabilities in what it calls the largest known distillation attack to date. Then Alibaba sued the Pentagon over its blacklist. The AI cold war between the US and China is now playing out in lawsuits and alleged model theft, which is the exact nation-state-capture risk that makes uncensorable decentralized AI matter. When the frontier models are being stolen, walled, and litigated across borders, the case for permissionless compute writes itself.

Chainlink joined 47 banks for T+0 settlement. After a month of me having nothing new to say about it, that is an actual catalyst. 47 banks using the infrastructure for instant settlement. The integration finally has teeth. LINK $7.45, still bleeding.

Walking out of that event, I felt pumped. So many real institutions are building now. Maybe the days of getting rich fast off this are over, but our technology is taking over, and I would rather be early to that than early to another memecoin.

Deeper dive into data: In other data this morning, the third and last government estimate for first quarter gross domestic product (GDP) surprisingly jumped to 2.1% from 1.6%. Analysts had expected no change. In less positive news, the government revised first quarter personal consumption in the GDP report down to 0.5% from the initial 1.4% estimate. The 0.5% increase was its smallest annualized quarterly increase since the first quarter of 2022. "The upward revision to overall GDP should come with an asterisk since the consumer tends to be a big driver of the economy, and it's not good news that personal consumption was lower than initially expected," Martin said. Additionally, the GDP deflator, which measures prices across the economy, rose 3.6% last quarter, above the 3.5% second estimate. Weekly initial jobless claims fell to 215,000, below the 225,000 consensus estimate from https://t.co/dZVXpz58Uf. May personal spending and personal income both climbed 0.7%, but personal spending in April, was revised down to 0.4% from 0.5% in April. Treasury note yields, already at one-month lows, extended their decline after the data.

Micron earnings and early chip action: Micron's earnings appeared to make a good impression as shares of the $1 trillion memory chip firm surged 18% ahead of the open. Its strength translated into positive performance for other chip stocks after two sessions of struggles. Checking the data, Micron's earnings per share of $25.11 easily beat consensus of $20.83 while revenue of $41.46 billion topped consensus of $35.85 billion. Gross margin of 84.9% was up sequentially, and the company cited "rapidly growing demand." Though Micron's results revived the chip sector this morning after the "great chip dip" earlier this week, one solid earnings report doesn't necessarily negate factors that drove the recent unwinding of crowded positioning. Concerns remain about returns on massive AI investments, concentration of investor money in chip, hyperscaler, and AI-related names, and the possible slowing of compute demand, said Liz Ann Sonders, chief investment strategist at SCFR.

Fed rate hike not certain: The U.S. dollar index hit new 2026 highs Wednesday, supported by ideas the Federal Reserve could hike rates at least once this year. It's fully priced in by October, and there's a rising implied volatility that one could come in September. That's far from assured. "We are not there yet—we need to see how the inflation outlook evolves over the next few months before we become more certain a hike is likely," Martin said. Last week's Fed statement made it clear that the committee favors its inflation mandate over its labor market mandate right now given recent strength in labor. Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh repeatedly stated that the committee was "unambiguous and unanimous" in its commitment to bringing inflation down. The updated "dot plot" of rate expectations also showed that nine participants now project a hike this year. That' doesn't really match the comments from Fed officials over the last month or so where there didn't seem to be an urgency to hike. Inflation trends may become more favorable with the recent drop in oil. In another development, the Fed's annual bank stress test confirmed banks are well positioned to weather a severe recession and continue lending to households and businesses. Banks often announce dividend increases after passing this test.

​Following the incident, the UN’s International Maritime Organization temporarily paused its framework to safely guide thousands of stranded seafarers out of the Gulf. Kinumpirma ni Kalihim ng Estado ng Estados Unidos na si Marco Rubio na hindi nakapagtamo ng anumang kasunduan ang Russia at Estados Unidos upang wakasan ang digmaan ng Russia sa Ukraine sa panahon ng Alaska Summit noong Agosto 2025. [1] Sinabi ni Rubio noong Hunyo 25 na mayroon lamang "panukala" sa Anchorage ngunit hindi nakapagtulungan ang mga panig sa panahon ng Summit. Binanggit ni Rubio na kabilang sa panukala ng Russia ang kontrol ng Russia sa Donbas at iba pang mga kahilingan. Si Rubio mismo ay bahagi ng diplomatikong delegasyon ng Estados Unidos sa Anchorage at dumalo sa mataas na antas na pagpupulong sa pagitan ni Pangulong Donald Trump ng Estados Unidos at ni Pangulong Vladimir Putin ng Russia. [2] Inangkin ni Putin noong Hunyo 23 na makikipagnegosasyon lamang ang Russia batay sa diumano'y mga kasunduan mula sa Alaska Summit kasama ang Estados Unidos, na itinanggi ni Rubio na umiiral.[3] Madalas gamitin ng mga opisyal ng Russia ang diumano'y "mga kasunduan sa Anchorage" upang itaguyod ang mga salaysay tungkol sa kahandaan ng Russia na makipagnegosasyon, sa kabila ng kawalan ng pampublikong pahayag kasunod ng summit.
 [4]
Ang naratibo ng kognitibong digmaan ng Russia na naglalayong ipakita na nagkakabagsak ang mga harapan ng digmaan sa Ukraine ay tila nabigong hikayatin ang mga katuwang ng Ukraine na sumuko sa mga kahilingan ng Russia. Sinabi nina Pangulong Donald Trump ng Estados Unidos at ng Pangalawang Kalihim ng Estado ng Estados Unidos na si Jeremy Levin noong Hunyo 24 at 25 na kasalukuyang may kalamangan ang Ukraine sa larangan ng digmaan, na nagpapakita na nabigo ang Russia na kumbinsihin ang Estados Unidos na nagkakabagsak ang mga harapan ng digmaan sa Ukraine.
 [5] Binigyang-diin ni Levin noong Hunyo 24 na ang malalayong pag-atake ng Ukraine sa imprastruktura ng langis ng Russia ay nagpapataas ng gastos ng digmaan para sa Russia at pinipilit ang Russia na ilipat ang mga mapagkukunan mula sa mga unahan ng labanan. Regular na gumagawa ng mga pahayag ang mga opisyal ng Kremlin, pinapalaki ang taktikal na pag-usad ng Russia, at nagpapakita ng maling ebidensya bilang bahagi ng mas malawak na pagsisikap sa kognitibong digmaan na subukang ipakita na gumuho ang mga unahan ng Ukraine – isang koordinadong kampanya upang pilitin ang Ukraine at ang mga kasosyo nito na sumuko sa mga kahilingan ng Russia sa negosasyon. [6] Ang matagumpay na kampanya ng Ukraine sa pag-atake sa malalayong at katamtamang distansya laban sa mga pag-aari ng militar ng Russia at imprastruktura ng enerhiya sa sinakop na Ukraine at Russia, kasama ang taktikal na pag-usad ng Ukraine sa ilang direksyon sa Taglamig at Tagsibol 2026, ay sumisira sa maling salaysay ng Russia na ipinapakita ang tagumpay ng Russia bilang hindi maiiwasan.
 [7]
Ang patuloy na kampanya ng pag-atake ng Ukraine laban sa mga refinery ng Russia ay nagpapalala sa mas malawak na presyur ng implasyon sa Russia at pinapahirap ang mga pagsisikap ng Kremlin na magsagawa ng palawig na patakarang pananalapi. Iniulat ng Bloomberg noong Hunyo 24, na iniuugnay sa Russian Federal Statistics Service, na tumaas ng tatlong porsyento ang presyo ng gasolina sa Russia hanggang $0.95 USD kada litro sa pagitan ng Hunyo 16 at 22, ang pinakamalaking lingguhang pagtaas sa loob ng hindi bababa sa 20 taon.
 [8] Iniulat ng Bloomberg na ang mga pag-atake ng Ukraine sa pinakamalalaking planta ng gasolina ng Russia ay nagtulak sa mga Ruso na mag-panic-buy ng gasolina, na nagdulot ng kakulangan at pagtaas ng presyo. Ayon sa isang pinagkakatiwalaang pinagkukunan ng datos sa Bloomberg, bumaba ng 15 porsyento ang produksyon ng gasolina ng Russia mula Hunyo 2025 at ng siyam na porsyento mula Mayo 2026, marahil dahil sa mas pinalakas na pag-atake ng Ukraine sa imprastruktura ng langis ng Russia.
 
Iniulat ng Russian opposition outlet na Verstka noong Hunyo 25 na ang malawakang kakulangan sa gasolina ay nakakaapekto sa lahat ng pederal na saklaw ng Rusya (mga rehiyon) maliban sa Ingushetia, Chechnya, Kalmykia, Chukotka, at ang Nenets Autonomous Okrug, bagaman tumaas ang mga presyo sa buong bansa.[9] Iniulat ng Bloomberg na ang epekto ng implasyon mula sa pagtaas ng presyo ng gasolina ay sumisingaw sa iba pang mga sektor dahil sa pagtaas ng gastos sa produksyon at transportasyon.
 
Iniulat ng Bloomberg na ayon sa datos ng Kagawaran ng Ekonomiya ng Russia, tumaas ang kabuuang taunang implasyon mula 5.3 porsyento hanggang 5.8 porsyento noong Hunyo 2026. Maaaring makagambala ang pangkalahatang presyur ng implasyon sa patuloy na pagbawas ng Bangko Sentral ng Russia sa pangunahing interest rate.
 
Pababa nang pababa ng Russian Central Bank ang pangunahing interest rate nito nang siyam na beses mula Hunyo 2025, mula 21 porsyento hanggang 14.25 porsyento, habang sinisikap ng Russia na dagdagan ang kapital na magagamit para sa Russian defense industrial base (DIB) at mapanatili ang itsura ng katatagan ng ekonomiya sa loob ng bansa.[10]
 
Inamin ni Gobernador ng Bangko Sentral ng Russia na si Elvira Nabiullina noong Hunyo 19 na ang pagtaas ng presyo ng gasolina sa loob ng bansa ay nakaapekto sa antas ng implasyon ng Hunyo at maaaring magtaas ng mga inaasahan sa implasyon, na nagpapahiwatig na maaaring kailanganin ng Bangko Sentral na baguhin ang kanilang pagkalkula tungkol sa pangunahing interest rate kung magpapatuloy ang mga pag-atake ng Ukraine sa imprastruktura ng enerhiya ng Russia at ang implasyon.[11]
 
Nagpataw ang Kremlin ng malaking presyon sa Bangko Sentral ng Russia at winasak ang kalayaan nito upang ibaba ang pangunahing interest rate habang sinisikap nitong ipagpatuloy ang pagpapalawak ng kapital para sa industriya ng depensa sa kabila ng mga palawakin na patakarang piskal at kakulangan sa gasolina na nagpapalala sa implasyon ng Russia.[12] Malamang na hihigit pa ang pang-ekonomiyang epekto ng kakulangan sa gasolina ng Russia kaysa sa mismong kakulangan sa gasolina kapag naayos na ng Russia ang problema.
 
Nagsimula ang kakulangan sa gasolina sa Russia noong Mayo 2026 at kumalat na sa karamihan ng mga rehiyon ng Russia at nagdulot ng pagtaas ng implasyon sa loob lamang ng ilang linggo.[13] Malamang na ipagpapatuloy ng mga puwersang Ukrainiano ang pagpapalakas ng kanilang kampanyang pag-atake laban sa imprastruktura ng langis ng Russia, lalo pang magpapalala ng kakulangan, at magpapataas ng presyur ng implasyon sa buong Russia.
 Ang pinalawak na presyur ng implasyon dahil sa krisis sa suplay ng gasolina sa Russia ay maaaring magpataas ng pagtutol ng Central Bank sa pagbaba ng pangunahing interest rate, na nagpapalito sa pagsisikap ng Kremlin para sa pagpapalawak ng mga patakarang pananalapi at lumilikha ng tensiyong administratibo.

Malamang ay patuloy na lumilikha ng mga bagong pormasyon sa papel ang komando militar ng Russia, ngunit hindi pa rin malinaw kung paano o kung magagawa ng Russia na punuan ang mga pormasyong ito ayon sa itinakdang lakas. Sinabi ni Punong Hepe ng Hukbong Ukrainiano Heneral Oleksandr Syrskyi noong Hunyo 25 na "inaayos ng komando militar ng Russia ang kanilang mga plano" at ngayon ay balak nitong bumuo ng hindi tinukoy na bilang ng mga bagong dibisyon at limang bagong brigada sa 2026.
 [14] 
Hindi malinaw kung tinutukoy ni Syrskyi ang isang karagdagang dibisyon at limang bagong brigada o kung tinutukoy niya ang patuloy na pagbuo ng mga pormasyon na inanunsyo ng komando militar ng Russia noong 2022 bilang bahagi ng mas malawak na repormang militar. 
Inanunsyo ng dating Ministro ng Depensa ng Russia na si Sergei Shoigu noong Disyembre 2022 na balak ng komando militar ng Russia na bumuo ng 17 bagong dibisyong pangmanobra, kabilang ang pagpapalawak ng limang umiiral na brigada ng pandagat na infanteriya tungo sa limang dibisyon, at kalaunan ay inanunsyo na balak ng Russia na itatag ang mga bagong pormasyong ito sa pagitan ng 2023 at 2026.[15]
 
Muling itinatag ng komando militar ng Russia ang mga distrito militar ng Moscow at Leningrad mula sa dating Distrito Militar ng Kanluran bilang bahagi ng mga repormang ito.[16] Nakabuo at nakapuwesto ang komando militar ng Russia ng hindi bababa sa 10 bagong dibisyon ng Russia para makipaglaban mula pa noong Disyembre 2022, bagaman wala sa mga nominasyong bagong pormasyong ito ang gumagana sa kanilang doktrinal na lakas.[17]
 
Bumuo ang komando militar ng Rusya ng ika-104 na Dibisyon ng Airborne (VDV); binago ang mga dati nang umiiral na brigada para maging ika-27, ika-68, ika-69, at ika-71 na dibisyong motorisadong riple; binago ang dalawang umiiral na brigada ng pandagat na infanteriya para maging ika-55 at ika-120 na dibisyong Pandagat na Infanteriya; at binuo ang ika-67, ika-70, at ika-72 na dibisyong motorisadong riple mula pa noong Disyembre 2022.
 
Iniulat ng Ukrainian military observer na si Kostyantyn Mashovets noong unang bahagi ng 2026 na apat na bagong dibisyon lamang mula sa 17 na planadong dibisyong pangmanobra at hanggang siyam na brigada ang nabuo ng komando militar ng Russia, na planong buuin noong 2025.[18]
 
Ayon sa ulat, plano rin ng kumand ng militar ng Russia na reorganisahin ang kanilang ika-810 na Hiwalay na Brigada ng Pandagat na Infanteriya bilang isang dibisyon ng pandagat na infanteriya, na magiging ika-labing-isang bagong dibisyong pangmaniobra na nabuo ng Russia mula pa noong 2022.[19]
 
Ang komando militar ng Russia ay lumilikha ng mga bagong pormasyon nang pangalan at sa papel lamang, dahil ang taktika nitong nakasandig sa infanteriya at ang mga hadlang sa ekonomiya at pagre-recruit ay malamang na makahahadlang sa komando militar ng Russia na epektibong ipatupad ang mga repormang ito at punuan nang buo ang mga bagong pormasyon habang nagiging aktibo ang mga ito.
 [20]
Kinumpiska ng mga awtoridad ng Pransya ang isang oil tanker ng tinagong flotang Ruso noong Hunyo 25. Iniulat ni Pangulong Emmanuel Macron ng Pransya na hinarang ng Hukbong Dagat ng Pransya ang oil tanker ng tinagong flotang Ruso na Deliver noong Hunyo 25 sa baybayin ng Sicily bilang paglabag sa batas pandagat.[21] 
Ang datos mula sa Automatic Identification Systems (AIS) ng Starboard Maritime Intelligence ship-tracking platform ay nagpapahiwatig na ang Deliver ay lumulutang sa ilalim ng banderang Kamerun at umalis sa Pantalan ng Primorsk noong Hunyo 4 at pinatay ang AIS tracking nito noong Hunyo 23. Ang mga estadong Europeo ay lalong nang-aagaw ng mga tangker ng anino ng flota ng Russia nitong mga nakaraang buwan bilang pagsisikap na pahinain ang kakayahan ng Russia na maghatid at kumita mula sa langis na nasanksyonan.
 [22]
Mga Pangunahing Tala
Kinumpirma ni Kalihim ng Estado ng Estados Unidos Marco Rubio na hindi nakapagtamo ng anumang kasunduan ang Russia at ang Estados Unidos upang wakasan ang digmaan ng Russia sa Ukraine sa panahon ng Alaska Summit noong Agosto 2025.
Ang naratibo ng Russia sa cognitive warfare na naglalayong ipakita na bumabagsak ang mga harapan ng digmaan sa Ukraine ay tila nabigong hikayatin ang mga katuwang ng Ukraine na sumuko sa mga kahilingan ng Russia.Ang patuloy na kampanya ng pag-atake ng Ukraine laban sa mga refinery ng Russia ay nagpapalala sa mas malawak na presyur ng implasyon sa Russia at pinapahirap ang mga pagsisikap ng Kremlin na magsagawa ng palawakin na patakarang pananalapi.
Malamang na patuloy na lumilikha ang komando militar ng Russia ng mga bagong yunit sa papel, ngunit hindi pa malinaw kung paano o kung magagawa ng Russia na punan ang mga yunit na ito ayon sa itinakdang lakas.Sinamsam ng mga awtoridad ng Pransya ang isang oil tanker ng tinagong flota ng Russia noong Hunyo 25.
Ipinagpatuloy ng mga puwersang Ukrainiano ang kanilang kampanyang pag-atake sa malalayong distansya laban sa imprastraktura ng langis ng Russia sa loob mismo ng Russia.
Nagpapaputok ang mga puwersang Ruso ng isang Iskander-M ballistic missile at 90 drone laban sa Ukraine sa magdamag.
Hindi kami nag-uulat nang detalyado tungkol sa mga krimen sa digmaan ng Russia dahil ang mga aktibidad na ito ay malawakang tinatalakay na sa mga midyang Kanluranin at hindi direktang nakakaapekto sa mga operasyong militar na aming sinusuri at tinataya. Magpapatuloy kaming suriin at mag-ulat sa mga epekto ng mga kriminal na aktibidad na ito sa militar ng Ukraine at sa populasyong Ukrainiano at partikular sa pakikipaglaban sa mga urban na lugar ng Ukraine. Lubos naming kinokondena ang mga paglabag ng Russia sa mga batas ng armadong tunggalian at sa mga Konbensiyon ng Geneva at ang mga krimen laban sa sangkatauhan kahit na hindi namin ito inilalarawan sa mga ulat na ito. 
Mga Operasyon ng Ukraine sa Pederasyong Ruso
Nagpatuloy ang mga puwersa ng Ukraine sa kanilang kampanyang pag-atake sa malalayong distansya laban sa imprastruktura ng langis ng Russia.
 Iniulat ng Pangkalahatang-Himpilan ng Ukraine noong Hunyo 25 na sinalakay ng mga puwersang Ukrainiano ang Poltavska Oil Depot sa Krasnodar Krai, na nagdulot ng sunog.[23] Binanggit ng Pangkalahatang-Himpilan ng Ukraine na ang depot ay nagbibigay ng langis sa mga puwersang Ruso sa sinakop na Ukraine. Ipinapakita ng mga geolocated na video ang usok at apoy sa depot ng langis.[24] Inamin ng mga opisyal ng Krasnodar Krai na ang mga pag-atake ng Ukraine ang naging sanhi ng sunog sa depot ng langis. [25] Iniulat ng Serbisyo ng Seguridad ng Ukraine (SBU) noong Hunyo 25 na pinuntirya ng mga puwersang Ukrainiano ang mga refinery ng Bashneft-Novoil at Bashneft-Ufanaftokhim sa Ufa, Republika ng Bashkortostan (mahigit 1,500 kilometro mula sa harapan ng labanan), na nagdulot ng sunog sa parehong pangunahing pasilidad ng AVT.[26] Ipinapakita ng mga geolocated na bidyo na inilathala noong Hunyo 25 ang mga sunog sa parehong refinery.
 [27]
Nagbigay ang mga sangguniang Ukrainiano ng na-update na pagtataya ng pinsala sa labanan (BDA) ng mga kamakailang pag-atake ng Ukraine. Iniulat ng Pangkalahatang Kawani ng Ukraine noong Hunyo 25 na ang pag-atake ng Ukraine noong Hunyo 22 laban sa Vladimir Space Communications Center sa Vladimir Oblast ay malubhang nagdulot ng pinsala sa pangunahing 25-metrong parabolic na antena, isang antena sa bubong, ang gitnang bahagi ng pangunahing hardware-software complex, at sa Hardware-Technical Building Blg. 1. [28] Iniulat ng Pangkalahatang Estado Mayor ng Ukraine na ang pag-atake ng Ukraine noong Hunyo 24 laban sa Orenburg Gas Processing Plant sa Orenburg Oblast ay nagdulot ng pinsala sa apat na yunit ng pagproseso ng gas at itinigil ang produksyon sa pasilidad.
Inanunsyo ng Freedom of Russia Legion (LSR) ng Pangunahing Direktorato ng Militar na Intelihensiya ng Ukraine (GUR) noong Hunyo 24 na inilunsad nito ang Operation Torch laban sa imprastruktura ng gas ng Russia. Ipinahayag ng LSR noong Hunyo 24 na tinamaan nito ang anim na pasilidad ng pamamahagi ng gas sa mga oblast ng Moscow at Tver at malubhang napinsala ang imprastruktura ng enerhiya, na nagdulot ng pagkagambala sa operasyon ng mga indibidwal na pasilidad.[29] Ipinahayag ng LSR na ilang buwan na nilang inihahanda ang Operation Torch bilang pagsisikap na higit pang pahinain ang sektor ng enerhiya ng Russia at ubusin ang kita mula sa langis.
Suportang Pagsusumikap ng Russia: Hilagang EjeLayunin ng Russia: Lumikha ng mga matatag na buffer zone sa Sumy Oblast sa kahabaan ng internasyonal na hangganan
Nagpatuloy ang mga puwersang Ruso sa mga opensibong operasyon sa hilagang Sumy Oblast noong Hunyo 24 at 25 ngunit hindi nakapagtala ng kumpirmadong pag-usad.[30] Inangkin ng isang Ruso milblogger na umatake ang mga puwersang Ruso sa timog ng Sopych, kanluran ng Chervona Zorya, at hilaga ng Sydorivka (lahat ay nasa hilagang-kanluran ng Lungsod ng Sumy).[31]

Pangunahing Pagsusumikap ng Russia: Silangang Ukraine
Pangunahing Pagsusumikap ng Russia na Pangalawa – Lalawigan ng Kharkiv
Layunin ng Russia: Itulak pabalik ang mga puwersang Ukrainiano mula sa internasyonal na hangganan upang makalikha ng isang matibay na buffer zone kasama ang Belgorod Oblast at makalapit sa saklaw ng tube artillery ng Lungsod ng Kharkiv
Nagpatuloy ang mga puwersang Ruso sa mga opensibong operasyon sa hilaga at hilagang-silangan ng Lungsod ng Kharkiv noong Hunyo 24 at 25 ngunit hindi sila nakausad.[32]

Nagpatuloy ang limitadong aktibidad sa lupa ng mga puwersang Ruso sa direksyon ng Velykyi Burluk noong Hunyo 25 ngunit hindi sila umusad.[33]
Nagpatuloy ang mga puwersang Ruso sa pag-atake sa imprastraktura ng enerhiya sa Kharkiv Oblast. Ipinapakita ng geolocated na bidyo na inilathala noong Hunyo 22 ang mga puwersang Ruso na nagsasagawa ng pag-atake gamit ang first-person view (FPV) drone laban sa isang transformer sa isang substation malapit sa Staryi Saltiv (silangan ng Lungsod ng Kharkiv). [34]
Pangalawang Pangunahing Pagsusumikap ng mga Ruso #2 – Ilog Oskil
Layunin ng mga Ruso: Tumaid sa Ilog Oskil sa Kharkiv Oblast at itulak pa-kanluran sa silangang Kharkiv Oblast at hilagang Donetsk Oblast
Nagpatuloy ang mga puwersang Ruso sa mga opensibong operasyon sa direksyon ng Kupyansk noong Hunyo 24 at 25 ngunit hindi sila nakausad. [35]
Nagpatuloy ang limitadong opensibong aktibidad ng mga puwersang Ruso sa direksyon ng Borova noong Hunyo 25 ngunit hindi sila nakausad.[36]
Nagpatuloy ang mga puwersang Ukrainiano sa kanilang kampanyang pag-atake sa katamtamang distansya laban sa mga pag-aari ng militar ng Russia at mga linya ng komunikasyon sa lupa (GLOCs) sa sinakop na Luhansk Oblast.
 Iniulat ng Pangkalahatang Estado Mayor ng Ukraine noong Hunyo 25 na sinalakay ng mga puwersang Ukrainiano ang dalawang tulay ng riles na nasa ibabaw ng mga ilog Aidar at Luhanchyk, na ginamit ng mga puwersang Ruso upang ilipat ang mga tropa at maghatid ng mga bala sa mga posisyon sa harapan ng labanan.[37] Iniulat ng Pangkalahatang Estado Mayor ng Ukraine na sinalakay din ng mga puwersang Ukrainiano ang isang bodega ng materyal at teknikal na kagamitan ng mga Ruso malapit sa sinakop na Novohannivka (mga 118 kilometro mula sa harapan ng labanan).[38]

Pangunahing Pagsusumikap #3 ng mga Ruso – Donetsk Oblast
Layunin ng mga Ruso: Sakupin ang buong Donetsk Oblast, ang inaangking teritoryo ng mga alipores ng Russia sa Donbas, at sumulong papaloob sa Dnipropetrovsk Oblast

Nagpatuloy ang mga puwersang Ruso sa mga opensibong operasyon sa direksyon ng Slovyansk noong Hunyo 24 at 25 ngunit hindi sila nakausad.[39] Ipinapakita ng geolocated na bidyo na inilathala noong Hunyo 25 ang pag-atake ng mga puwersang Ruso sa isang posisyon ng Ukraine sa timog ng Lyman, sa mga lugar na dati nang inaangkin ng mga pinagkukunang Ruso na pinananatilihan ng mga puwersang Ruso ang kanilang mga posisyon.
 [40]
Isinasagawa ng mga puwersang Ruso ang pag-atake gamit ang glide bomb laban sa mga posisyon ng Ukraine sa direksyon ng Slovyansk. Ipinapakita ng geolocated na bidyo na inilathala noong Hunyo 25 ang paggamit ng tatlong guided FAB-1500 glide bomb ng mga puwersang Ruso upang atakihin ang pansamantalang puwesto ng Ukraine na matatagpuan sa Slovyansk Thermal Power Plant sa Mykolaivka (silangan ng Slovyansk).
 [41] Inangkin ng Ministri ng Depensa ng Rusya (MoD) noong Hunyo 25 na nagsagawa ang mga puwersang Ruso ng pag-atake gamit ang FAB-500 glide bomb laban sa isang tulay sa ibabaw ng Ilog Siverskyi Donets malapit sa Mayaky (hilaga ng Slovyansk), naghulog ng anim na glide bomb sa mga umatras na puwersang Ukrainiano malapit sa Shchurove (timog-kanluran ng Lyman), at gumamit ng apat na FAB-500 glide bomb upang atakihin ang mga posisyon ng Ukrainiano sa kanlurang Lyman.[42] Isang Russian milblogger ang naglathala ng bidyo noong Hunyo 24 na diumano'y nagpapakita ng mga puwersang Ruso na nagsasagawa ng pag-atake gamit ang FAB-1500 glide bomb laban sa mga posisyon ng Ukrainiano malapit sa Vysokoivanivka (silangan ng Slovyansk).
 [43] Ang mga pinatinding pag-atake gamit ang glide bomb na ito ay malamang na bahagi ng isang kampanyang Russian battlefield air interdiction (BAI) na naglalayong maghanda ng kondisyon para sa mga opensibong operasyon laban sa Slovyansk at putulin ang lohistika ng Ukraine papuntang Lyman.
Aktibong ginagamit ng mga puwersang Ruso ang mga strike drone para tutukan ang lohistika ng Ukraine ngunit nahihirapan silang sumulong sa direksyon ng Lyman.
 Iniulat ng kumander ng isang batalyong Ukrainiano na nagpapatrolya sa direksyon ng Lyman noong Hunyo 25 na nananatiling matatag ang sitwasyon sa direksyon ng Lyman at hindi nakakamit ng mga puwersang Ruso ang taktikal na pag-usad.[44] Binanggit ng kumander na patuloy na aktibong ginagamit ng mga puwersang Ruso ang iba't ibang uri ng strike drone na kayang mag-operate nang mas malalim at maabot ang mga rutang lohistikal ng Ukraine sa tulong ng mga elemento ng elit na Russian Rubikon Center for Advanced Unmanned Technologies.
 Napansin ng kumander na hindi aktibong ginagamit ng mga puwersang Ruso ang mabibigat na bomber drone sa direksyon ng Lyman sa kasalukuyan.
Kamakailan ay nagsagawa ang mga puwersang Ruso ng isang misyon ng paglusob sa taktikal na lugar ng Kostyantynivka-Druzhkivka. Ipinapakita ng geolocated na bidyo na inilathala noong Hunyo 17 ang pag-atake ng mga puwersang Ukrainiano sa isang posisyon ng mga Ruso sa Kostyantynivka matapos ang tinaya ng ISW na isang misyon ng paglusob ng mga Ruso. [45]
Patuloy na ipinapalaganap ng Kremlin ang isang sistematikong pagsisikap sa kognitibong digmaan na nag-aangkin ng malawakang pag-usad ng Russia sa Kostyantynivka. Noong Hunyo 25, ipinagpatuloy ng Kagawaran ng Tanggulang Ruso (MoD) ang kamakailang gawi nito ng paglalathala ng sobrang detalyadong pekeng ulat tungkol sa mga nakamit ng Russia sa Kostyantynivka upang palakihin ang mga tagumpay ng Russia.
 [46] Naglathala rin ang TASS, ang newswire ng Kremlin, noong Hunyo 25 ng isang panayam sa pangalawang kumander ng Unang Batalyon ng Rusya ng ika-1194 na Rehimentong Motorized Rifle (ika-4 na Separate Motorized Rifle Brigade, ika-3 Hukbong Pinagsama-samang Sandata [CAA], dating ika-2 Korps ng Hukbong Bayan ng Luhansk [LNR AC],
 Southern Military District [SMD]) na naglalarawan ng inaangking pag-usad sa Kostyantynivka.[47] Ang mga patuloy na pag-angkin na ito ay bahagi ng pagsisikap ng Kremlin sa digmaang kognitibo na naglalayong magpalabis sa pag-usad ng Russia sa buong harapan ng labanan, na salungat sa lahat ng magagamit na ebidensya.[48]
1/2

text: @CBSNews IRAN JUST HIT A CARGO SHIP AND OIL TANKER WITH DRONES DAMAGING THEM  IN HORMUZ THAT WERE NOT APPROVED BY IRANS IRGC!!!! ONE SANK.

The threadbare cliché that still frames Western media analysis shows Washington as the absolute power and Tehran as a pleading supplicant. 
Yet this narrative is in total contradiction with the reality on the ground.

West Asia, once America’s backyard, has today become an arena where the equations are decided in the Strait of Hormuz and amid the wreckage of burned and destroyed American bases.

The Strait of Hormuz is no longer an ordinary waterway; it is the beating heart of the global economy, now held entirely in the grip of Iran’s control. No oil tanker dares pass without Iran’s permission and a visa. Iran has effectively acquired a real, hardware-based veto over the world’s vital artery.

America’s military hegemony in the region is not merely shaken, it has collapsed in a humiliating fashion. Key bases that once symbolized absolute power have been turned into vulnerable, devastated targets by Iran’s precision missile and drone strikes. American command centers and air defenses now lie directly in Tehran’s line of fire, and their former deterrence has become a joke.

The bitter and undeniable truth is that America, like it or not, has no choice but to submit to the new regional order dominated by Iran. For Washington, negotiations are not an opportunity to shift the balance but merely a humiliating formality to officially recognize the balance that Iran has imposed on the battlefield.

From Tehran to Beirut and Sanaa, Iran today is the undisputed power of the region, rewriting the rules of the game at will, a fact clearly visible even in the opening clause of the MOU.

In the next 30 to 60 days, America has only two options: either realistically accept this Iranian hegemony and engage in a gradual retreat, or play with fire and create tensions in which Iran, leveraging its total control of the Strait of Hormuz and its capability to completely destroy remaining bases and facilities, will push the entire region to the brink.

From this perspective, the drawdown of oil inventories is no longer a simple economic matter; it is a blunt military warning from Tehran to the entire world that global energy security is henceforth dependent on Iran’s will.

The Americans may still be deluded by sanctions and pressure, but the battlefield has clearly swapped the roles. The only remaining path is the coerced acceptance of this reality: America is no longer the region’s policeman, and the post-American Middle East has officially entered the sphere of Iranian influence and dominance.

Any attempt to revive the old order is not only doomed to fail but is also a dangerous self-deception, the heavy and devastating cost of which will be paid by the global economy, America, and its mercenary and client regimes.
